Query for account memberships count

Required account permissions: `read:account_membership`

Get an Account Membership by user id.

Looks up the membership for the given user within the account and returns
enriched member details including timezone and team memberships.

Returns 404 when the user is not a member of the account (e.g. they have
since been removed), allowing the frontend to show a "deactivated user"
fallback.

Required account permissions: `read:account_membership`

Get an Account Membership by bot id.

Looks up the membership for the given bot within the account and returns
enriched member details including team memberships.

Returns 404 when the bot is not a member of the account.

Required account permissions: `read:account_membership`

Get an Account Membership by membership id.

Returns enriched member details including timezone and team memberships.
Returns 404 for bot memberships — use the bots endpoint for those.

Required account permissions: `read:account_membership`

Update an account membership by id.

Required account permissions: `update:account_membership`

Delete an account membership by id.
This will also delete any workspace accesses that the membership's actor
has under the account.

Required account permissions: `delete:account_membership`
Required account permissions: `update:account_membership`
Required account permissions: `update:account_membership`

Retrieves information about the managed execution usage and terms for a given workspace.
This includes the total compute time in seconds utilized by the workspace during the current billing period,
as well as limits pertaining to the account terms, such as concurrency, work pool, and compute limits.

Returns:
    WorkspaceLimitsUsageResponse: An object containing the compute usage in seconds and limits on
    concurrency, work pool, and compute usage for the specified workspace.

Check whether the account is currently exceeding any rate limits.

Returns a list of bucket names that are currently exceeded. The frontend
uses this to display a warning banner. The exceeded status auto-clears
~15 minutes after the last denied request.

Required account permissions: `read:account`

Record a batch of Events for an Account

Queries for Events matching the given filter criteria in the given Account.  Returns
the first page of results, and the URL to request the next page (if there are more
results).

Returns the next page of Events for a previous query against the given Account, and
the URL to request the next page (if there are more results).

Returns distinct objects and the count of events associated with them.  Objects
that can be counted include the day the event occurred, the type of event, the
workspace, or the IDs of the resources associated with the event.
Record a batch of Events for one Workspace within an Account

Queries for Events matching the given filter criteria in the given Account and
Workspace.  Returns the first page of results, and the URL to request the next
page (if there are more results).

Returns the next page of Events for a previous query against the given Account and
Workspace, and the URL to request the next page (if there are more results).

Returns distinct objects and the count of events associated with them.  Objects
that can be counted include the day the event occurred, the type of event, the
workspace, or the IDs of the resources associated with the event.

params.name: limit
params.description: Defaults to PREFECT_ORION_API_DEFAULT_LIMIT if not provided.

items.docs:
Returns the first page of Events for a flow run against the given Account and Workspace.

If the `if-none-match` header is provided with the value of the current event count,
the server will return a 304 Not Modified response since the client already has the
latest events.

settings. Enqueues a fan-out docket task; individual workspaces are
processed asynchronously.

items.docs:
Get observed logger names used in a specific trace.

Returns distinct logger names used in the trace with their usage counts
and first/last seen timestamps. Useful for showing what loggers were
active in a flow run and providing autocomplete/filtering options.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    trace_id: The trace (flow run) ID to analyze
    query: Optional prefix search on logger name
    order_by: Order by name (alphabetically) or recency (most recent first)
    limit: Maximum numb

items.docs:
Get observed logger names for a workspace.

Returns distinct logger names across all traces in the workspace.
Results are filtered by workspace retention policy.
Useful for providing autocomplete/filtering options in the UI.

Args:
    account_id: The account ID
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    query: Optional prefix search on logger name
    order_by: Order by name (alphabetically) or recency (most recent first)
    limit: Maximum number of results

Returns:
    List of ObservedLogger obj

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

Bulk delete flows using a filter.

This endpoint soft-deletes flows matching the provided filter criteria,
then hard-deletes their associated deployments.
Flow runs are soft-deleted asynchronously in the background.
Associated automations are cleaned up asynchronously in the background.

If ANY deployment for a flow is unauthorized, the entire request fails
and nothing is deleted.

Required workspace scopes: `manage_flows`

Required workspace scopes: `run_flows`

Bulk delete flow runs using a filter.

This endpoint soft-deletes flow runs matching the provided filter criteria.
For small numbers of flow runs (5 or fewer), cleanup is performed synchronously.
For larger batches, cleanup is queued as a background job.

Required workspace scopes: `manage_flows`

Bulk set state on flow runs matching a filter.

This endpoint attempts to set the specified state on all flow runs
matching the filter criteria. Each flow run is processed through
orchestration rules (unless force=True), so individual transitions
may be accepted, rejected, aborted, or waited.

The response body contains detailed per-run results indicating
the outcome for each flow run.

Required workspace scopes: `run_flows`

Set a flow run state, invoking any orchestration rules.

info.name: Get Scheduled Flow Runs For Deployments

items.docs:
Get scheduled runs for a set of deployments. Used by a runner to poll for work.

Required workspace scopes: `see_deployments`

Count deployments.

Required workspace scopes: `see_deployments`

Bulk delete deployments using a filter.

This endpoint deletes deployments matching the provided filter criteria.
Deployments are deleted immediately so they disappear from the UI.
Associated automations are cleaned up asynchronously in the background.

If ANY deployment in the filter is unauthorized, the entire request fails
and nothing is deleted.

Required workspace scopes: `manage_deployments`

Schedule runs for a deployment. For backfills, provide start/end times in the past.

This function will generate the minimum number of runs that satisfy the min
and max times, and the min and max counts. Specifically, the following order
will be respected.

    - Runs will be generated starting on or after the `start_time`
    - No more than `max_runs` runs will be generated
    - No runs will be generated after `end_time` is reached
    - At least `min_runs` runs will be generated
    - Runs wi

Resume the deployment. Runs will be scheduled immediately.

Required workspace scopes: `write_deployments`

Resume the deployment. Runs will be scheduled immediately.

Required workspace scopes: `write_deployments`

Set a deployment schedule to inactive. Any auto-scheduled runs still in a Scheduled
state will be deleted.

Required workspace scopes: `write_deployments`

Set a deployment schedule to inactive. Any auto-scheduled runs still in a Scheduled
state will be deleted.

Required workspace scopes: `write_deployments`

Create a flow run from a deployment.

Any parameters not provided will be inferred from the deployment's parameters.
If tags are not provided, the deployment's tags will be used.

If no state is provided, the flow run will be created in a SCHEDULED state with
a scheduled start time of NOW.

Required workspace scopes: `run_deployments`

info.name: Bulk Create Flow Runs From Deployment

items.docs:

Each item in the list uses the same schema as the single create endpoint.
Results are returned in the same order as the input list.

Get list of work-queues that are able to pick up the specified deployment.

This endpoint is intended to be used by the UI to provide users warnings
about deployments that are unable to be executed because there are no work
queues that will pick up their runs, based on existing filter criteria. It
may be deprecated in the future because there is not a strict relationship
between work queues and deployments.

params.name: trace_id
params.description: The trace ID to query spans for

params.name: parent_span_id
params.description: Filter by parent span ID (None = all spans)

params.name: depth
params.description: Filter by depth level (None = all depths)

params.name: tags
params.description: Filter by tags (OR logic - span matches if it has ANY of the specified tags)

params.name: query
params.description: Text search query across span content (space=OR, -/!=NOT, quotes=phrases)

items.docs:
Get spans with cursor-based pagination for efficient browsing.

Use cases:
- Initial page load (depth=0 for root spans)
- Expand a node (parent_span_id=X for children)
- Browse all spans at specific depth
- Filter by state types/names
- Paginate through large result sets (both directions)

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

params.name: span_id
params.description: The target span to get context for

params.name: context_size
params.description: Number of siblings before/after to include

items.docs:
Get full context window for detailed span view.

Returns:
- spans: All ancestors + siblings in context window
- target_span_id: The requested span ID
- has_more_before/after: Pagination indicators

Use case: When user clicks on a span or loads a deep link URL

The frontend receives a flat list of spans and can:
- Build the tree structure from parent relationships
- Highlight the target span
- Show pagination controls based on has_more flags

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

params.name: trace_id
params.description: The trace ID to query observed states for

params.name: query
params.description: Prefix search query for state type or name

params.name: order_by
params.description: Order results by name (alphabetically) or recency (most recently seen first)

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

items.docs:
Get observed state type/name combinations used in a specific trace.

Returns distinct state combinations used in the trace.
Useful for showing what states were actually used in a flow run and
providing autocomplete/filtering options.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    trace_id: The trace (flow run) ID to analyze
    query: Optional prefix search on state_type or state_name
    order_by: Order by name (alphabetically) or recency (most recent first)
    limit: Maximum number of results

params.name: trace_id
params.description: The trace ID to query observed tags for

params.name: query
params.description: Prefix search query for tag names

items.docs:
Get all observed tags and their counts for a specific trace.

Returns all tags used in spans within the trace with their occurrence counts.
Useful for showing what tags were used in a flow run and their frequency.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    trace_id: The trace (flow run) ID to analyze

Returns:
    List of ObservedTag objects with tag names and counts

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

Get a single span by ID within a specific trace.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    trace_id: The trace (flow run) ID containing the span
    span_id: The span ID to retrieve

Returns:
    SpanResponse with has_children and child_count computed

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if span not found or doesn't belong to trace

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

params.name: ids
params.description: List of span IDs to retrieve. Only returns spans that exist and belong to the specified trace.

items.docs:
Get multiple spans by IDs within a specific trace.

Returns only spans that exist and belong to the specified trace.
Empty IDs list or no matches returns empty list.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    trace_id: The trace (flow run) ID containing the spans
    ids: List of span IDs to retrieve

Returns:
    List of SpanResponse objects with has_children and child_count computed

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if trace doesn't exist

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

params.name: run_id
params.description: The run ID to lookup span information for

items.docs:
Look up trace and span information for a given run ID.

Treats the run_id as a span_id and returns the trace_id and span_id
of the found span. This is useful for navigating from a child run ID
to its trace view.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    run_id: The run ID to look up (treated as span_id)

Returns:
    SpanIdentifierResponse containing trace_id and span_id

Raises:
    HTTPException: 404 if span not found

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

params.name: span_id
params.description: The span ID to query observed tags for

params.name: query
params.description: Prefix search query for tag names

items.docs:
Get all observed tags and their counts for a specific span and all its descendants.

Uses a recursive query to find all descendant spans, then aggregates tags
from the target span and all its children recursively.

Args:
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    span_id: The span ID to analyze (includes this span + all descendants)

Returns:
    List of ObservedTag objects with tag names and occurrence counts

Required workspace scopes: `see_flows`

params.name: query
params.description: Prefix search query for state type or name

params.name: order_by
params.description: Order results by name (alphabetically) or recency (most recently seen first)

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return

items.docs:
Get observed state type/name combinations for a workspace.

Returns distinct state combinations across all traces in the workspace.
Results are filtered by workspace retention policy.
Useful for providing autocomplete/filtering options in the UI.

Args:
    account_id: The account ID
    workspace_id: The workspace ID
    query: Optional prefix search on state_type or state_name
    order_by: Order by name (alphabetically) or recency (most recent first)
    limit: Maximum number of results
